column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) design-smell tells for audit-swiftui-design-review (dr-*).
# severity: hard | warn | adv. NONE are hard — design smells are not ship-blockers; the reviewer READS
# the rendered screenshot + code and judges against the cited KB before reporting (LOCATE-only).
# Structural cases grep can't express (FAB shape, icon-only-button containment) live in ast-grep/*.yml (optional).

column4: hardcoded font size — prefer a built-in text style (Body 17pt) so Dynamic Type works. hig-design-rubric.md#typography
hardcoded color — prefer semantic colors that adapt to Dark Mode / Increase Contrast. hig-design-rubric.md#color-contrast
text field — confirm .keyboardType + .textContentType match the field's semantics (email/number/password). ux-smell-catalog.md#forms-input
icon candidate — if it is the sole label of a Button/tappable with no .accessibilityLabel, VoiceOver reads the symbol name. hig-design-rubric.md#accessibility-visual
possible Android FAB — a floating circular shadowed button (esp. bottom-trailing) is non-idiomatic on iOS; put primary actions in the nav/tool bar. ux-smell-catalog.md#native-vs-not
hamburger glyph — if it hides primary navigation, iOS expects a persistent bottom tab bar instead. ux-smell-catalog.md#native-vs-not
fixed-size frame — confirm it doesn't clip/overlap at the largest Dynamic Type (AX5). hig-design-rubric.md#accessibility-visual
